entry.title: Sydney Morning Herald: 16 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: Sydney Morning Herald: 16 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-16 07:31AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Test rugby LIVE updates: All Blacks v Ireland, Wallabies v England
  • AFL 2022 round 18 LIVE updates: Massive upset brewing as North run riot against Tigers, Nankervis reported
  • War crimes in Ukraine - The Torture Cell
  • War crimes in Ukraine - The Missing
  • Roosters’ big win comes at bigger injury cost
  • Brisbane Lions make their top four case with important win over GWS Giants
  • ‘He will succeed’: Bennett backs Tigers’ Marshall plan
  • Why tens of thousands of flying foxes have taken over Tamworth
  • Albanese’s pandemic payment backflip shows the limits of the blame game
  • Collingwood perform another great escape to celebrate Pendlebury’s 350th
  • Surry Hills cottage in need of a reno snapped up for $1.35 million at hot auction
  • Pangai jnr vows to protect Burton if Tino tries it on again
  • Mexico captures infamous drug lord Rafael Caro Quintero
  • First home buyer beats investors for $771,000 Altona North house at auction
  • How grief spawned a gritty determination in parliament’s new umpire
  • North president contacts Clarkson’s manager to start coach hunt
  • Warning sirens sound in Kyiv as Russia steps up long-range bombardment
  • National cabinet reinstates COVID crisis payments, telehealth sessions
  • Best of cartoons, July 16, 2022
  • The latest illustrations from artist Alan Moir
  • Spanish pitch to supply Australia with three warships by end of 2030
  • While Albanese scores in footy diplomacy, that other state of origin – China – has an end game
  • Nurse job vacancies doubled during pandemic
  • The latest illustrations from artist Michael Leunig
  • The latest illustrations from artist Simon Letch
  • The latest illustrations from artist Matt Davidson
  • The latest illustrations from artist Matt Golding
  • Best of cartoons, July 15, 2022
  • The latest illustrations from artist Andrew Dyson
  • Albanese signs off on restoring the $750 pandemic isolation payment in policy reversal
  • One dead, another fighting for life after NSW house fires
  • Three charged with murder over stabbing death of teen at Royal Easter Show
  • Doctors demand end to ‘culture of cover up’ after string of patient deaths
  • Why private girls’ schools leader has switched to alternative co-ed
  • Supreme Court test case that could throw out thousands of NSW COVID fines
  • ‘Underwear flapping in the breeze’: Putting Sydney’s balconies to the nude test
  • Do the right thing and mask up to stop surge
  • From the Archives, 2004 : Aussie model wins Miss Universe
  • House votes to restore abortion rights, prohibit punishment
  • ‘He was’: Biden tells Saudi prince who was responsible for Khashoggi’s murder
  • Britons told to avoid alcohol, prepare for record ‘red alert’ heatwave
  • Spotlight back on church whose members were once known as ‘Moonies’
  • Why nobody wants to be in the office on Fridays
  • Ivana Trump died from ‘blunt force trauma injuries to her torso’
  • Inside the Ukraine war crimes investigation, Part 1: The Missing
  • War crimes in Ukraine - The Destruction
  • Fires, evacuations and health warnings as European temperatures hit 47 degrees
  • Queenslanders again urged to don masks indoors, as third COVID wave escalates
  • Gallery: The best photos from around the world
  • Victoria surpasses 800 hospitalisations, hits stage three of ‘winter plan’
  • Northern Territory’s dry zone laws come to an end
  • New memorial for six men killed in sunken trawler off Queensland coast
  • Ex WA police chief sworn in as governor
  • Why the City of Sydney pays for a British flag to fly near Circular Quay
  • Gail helps train men to do a job the Catholic Church won’t let her do
  • Safety or liberty? Welcome to the pandemic’s personal paradox phase
  • Beauty and the beast: Why Zoe Foster Blake is the least of BWX’s problems
  • Behind the scenes of Dry July with bars, breweries and bottle-Os
  • Pedigree London & Lancashire building hits the market
  • The Mill snapped up for $60m as pub sales hit new levels
  • Stuck, sunk, bogged or buried? Aussie Salvage Squad has the answer
  • Is laughter really the best medicine?
  • Ab Fab? Not quite, but at least this comedy offers more than great music
  • Il Trovatore provides vividness, mystery, colour and pressing relevance
  • Decades after NWA fought off censorship, Australia has declared its own war on hip-hop
  • From Seth Meyers to Liam Gallagher, Amyl and the Sniffers soak up global spotlight
  • ‘It’s been unbelievable’: The surprising success of Everything Everywhere All at Once
  • Going the extra $50m mile to recreate the Golden Mile
  • Opera House architect Peter Hall’s family upset at Concert Hall changes
  • ‘A complicated murder mystery’: What’s killing Sydney’s frogs?
  • So you want to host a COP? How to win the UN climate talks
  • Lock in 43 per cent emissions cut and then build on it
Breaking news from Sydney, Australia and the world. Features the latest business, sport, entertainment, travel, lifestyle, and technology news.

entry.title: Washington Post: 16 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Russia-Ukraine war live updates: Widespread air raid alerts as Russia intensifies strikes beyond Ukraine’s east
  • Md. churches’ youth group stuck at Panama compound amid political unrest
  • Mexico arrests Caro Quintero, drug lord convicted in death of DEA agent
  • Gang war traps thousands in Haitian slum
  • Up to 150 civilians killed in recent Russian strikes, Pentagon says
  • Outcry over U.S. visa delays after Kenyan sprinter nearly misses race
  • Doctors treating monkeypox complain of ‘daunting’ paperwork, obstacles
  • Biden says he confronted Saudis directly on Khashoggi
  • Two universities in Mykolaiv hit by missiles; Russian forces advance on Siversk
  • Economic worry keeps E.U. from imposing tougher sanctions on Russia
  • German soldier who posed as a refugee found guilty of attack plot
  • British man held by pro-Russian separatists has died, official says
  • China’s economic growth slows to 0.4%, weakest in two years
  • Biden backs Palestinian state, calls for accounting of journalist’s death
  • Griner’s defense documents medical cannabis prescription at trial
  • What inflation? China’s peppercorn purveyors are feeling the heat.
  • South Korean capital celebrates 1st Pride parade in 3 years
  • 'Bang, bang': Children live and play near Ukraine front line
  • Israel strikes Gaza target in response to rocket fire
  • Tropical Storm Estelle forms in Pacific off southern Mexico
  • Mexico's capture of drug kingpin could be signal to US
  • Guatemala returns 1st of its 21 victims from Texas trailer
  • The AP Interview: Sri Lanka candidate sees tough work ahead
  • Mexico captures infamous drug lord Rafael Caro Quintero
  • Rockets fired from Gaza into Israel hours after Biden visit
  • Jayland Walker was shot nearly four dozen times, autopsy results show
  • Record shows Indiana doctor fulfilled duty to report 10-year-old’s abortion
  • Texas sues Biden admin for requiring abortions in medical emergencies
  • Actress Constance Wu says she attempted suicide after Twitter backlash
  • One roommate is 85, the other is 27. Such arrangements are growing.
  • A Texas meteorologist warned of power outages. Then the lights went out.
  • Citing terrorism, U.S. seeks 15-year prison sentence in Jan. 6 case
  • Post Politics Now: Biden calls murder of Khashoggi ‘outrageous,’ says he raised it in meeting with Saudi crown prince
  • N.Y. men who pepper-sprayed officers on Jan. 6 sentenced to 44 months
  • After staff exodus, Vice President Harris to lose long-serving aide
  • Mixed messages on economy raises questions on recession risks
  • Amazon data center dispute led to strange government intervention
  • Inflation is soaring. Time to rethink your emergency savings.
  • Nobody wants to be in the office on Fridays
  • Groups that aid abortion patients pull back, fearing legal liability
  • The least diverse cities and most common statues in America. And more!
  • Musk fires back at Twitter suit in new court filing
  • ‘Apex Legends’ esports’ stadium debut is a rebirth, not a victory lap
  • Historical hunt country estate on the market for $27.5 million
  • Tories Must Decide Between Thatcher and Reagan Leadership
  • Data-Dependent Fed Runs Out of Data to Change Its Mind
  • Wells Fargo and Citi Customers Are Still Spending
  • Inflation Is Even Worse If You Measure It the Proper Way
  • The GOP courted antiabortion zealots. Now they’re stuck with them.
  • How Biden stood up to a bully
  • In South Carolina, a dark family tragedy. And now an arrest.
  • We need an American Girl doll who hasn’t given up
  • Trumpism makes the inflation fight urgent
  • When is a child not a child?
  • Gavin Newsom likely isn’t the answer Democrats are looking for
  • How Gabby Giffords found her voice again
  • In a close race, Texas’s Abbott talks war — against migrants
  • An emergency declaration on abortion rights could do more harm than good
  • Glenn Youngkin gives Virginia parents another victory
  • Leave Rock Creek Park’s upper Beach Drive closed to cars
  • Virginia’s clean-energy future is in peril
  • Want Maryland’s primary results? You’ll have to wait.
  • Maryland, we’re almost at the finish line, but don’t forget to vote.
  • Readers critique The Post: Explaining nil on NIL
  • Ask Eugene Robinson about the latest news around the U.S. and world
  • Chat with Perry Bacon Jr. about politics and beyond
  • Chat with Jennifer Rubin about her columns, politics, policy and more
  • Need a dose of humor in your day? Chat with Alexandra Petri.
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: NY Times: 16 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Joe Manchin Defends Retreat on Climate and Tax Plans
  • Biden Concedes Defeat on Climate Bill as Manchin and Inflation Upend Agenda

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 16 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 16 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-16 07:04AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Hong Kong protests: 2 jailed fugitives among 12 caught at sea on Taiwan flee bid hit with extra 21 months in prison over petrol bombs, deadly items
  • Coronavirus: should Hong Kong ease curbs and build natural Covid immunity through infections? Health chief to look into experts’ call but warns ‘policy doesn’t depend on theory’
  • New World First Bus fans vow to take photos and many rides before colours change to Citybus’ yellow and red following merger
  • London-based, anti-China group Friends of Hong Kong distances itself from members said to be involved in case of 4 protesters caught after 20 months in hiding
  • Time to ‘get away’: Hong Kong residents cooped up by Covid pandemic take flight as travel bug bites, never mind soaring airfares
  • Hong Kong phrase ‘add oil’ is older than you think – and we need it now more than ever
  • Enrolment slumps at Hong Kong’s direct subsidy scheme secondary schools, hit hardest by wave of emigration
  • Case of 4 Hong Kong protesters caught after 20 months in hiding: anti-China group members admit to helping them but deny taking their money
  • Uber to raise fares after Hong Kong government approves similar move by taxis, with adjustments set to kick in on Sunday
  • Reselling tickets to Hong Kong’s Palace Museum an offence, warns cultural hub district CEO, after asking third-party websites to take down listings
  • Hong Kong residents may only need utility bill to appeal rejection of application for HK$5,000 voucher scheme as government weighs simpler process
  • Hong Kong protests: 7 fugitives jailed for 10 months each for thwarted escape to Taiwan
  • US approves possible sale of military assistance to Taiwan worth US$108 billion
  • Chinese military upgrades near disputed Himalayan border viewed as provocative in India
  • ‘Too easy to borrow’: debt plagues young people despite China’s scrutiny of fintech microloans
  • China’s national carbon trading scheme marks one-year anniversary, with analysts expecting stricter regulation and data monitoring ahead
  • 5 unanswered questions about the Henan bank crisis
  • US-made HIMARS rockets are giving Ukraine an edge against Russia. Will Taiwan be next to benefit?
  • A great flood may have helped ancient Chinese civilisation expand southward, geologists say
  • China’s supply chain ‘irreplaceable’ despite Covid-19 disruption and expansion in India, Vietnam, says contract maker of Xiaomi smartphones
  • China GDP: second quarter plunge creates ‘impossible’ task to hit annual growth target without stimulus
  • China jobs: youth unemployment hits record high in June – nearly 1 in 5 young people out of work
  • China warns US against making ties worse and fuelling Asia-Pacific division
  • Shanghai’s Web3 support draws industry scepticism, but also ‘cautious optimism’ in country that banned cryptocurrencies
  • Chinese scientists find DNA link with Native American ancestors in Yunnan cave
  • Chinese bank scandal customers struggle to register for scheme to pay back money
  • China asks UN Security Council for ban on small arms to Haiti amid gang violence
  • ‘Treated like a virus’: Chinese teacher says she was sacked because she’d had Covid
  • Sri Lanka envoy ‘confident’ that China will agree to aid requests ‘at some point’
  • Chinese astronauts want the thinnest nappy in space: researchers
  • Is Fiji’s ‘Little India’ facing extinction?
  • Sri Lanka economic crisis: presidential nominee Sajith Premadasa vows to catch those who looted nation
  • Is Singapore pursuing a false climate solution by buying Mekong hydropower?
  • Facing energy crisis, Japanese public backs resumption of more nuclear reactors – as long as they are safe
  • Unarmed black man Jayland Walker was shot 46 times by Ohio police, autopsy shows
  • Ukraine says Russia using Europe’s largest nuclear plant to store missiles
  • Mexico captures drug lord Rafael Caro Quintero who killed US agent
  • Elon Musk opposes Twitter’s bid to speed up trial over US$44 billion deal
  • Biden finds oil thicker than blood as he sets aside Saudi prince snub amid Ukraine war
  • UK PM contenders clash over taxes in contest to replace Boris Johnson
  • UK PM race: Downing Street cat Larry drawn into political fight for his residence
  • Ukraine girl, 4, killed in Russian missile strike on city of Vinnytsia
  • Joe Biden confronts Saudi crown prince over Jamal Khashoggi murder, expects action on energy
  • US, Russia sign deal to share flights to International Space Station despite Ukraine tensions
  • Iran unveils drone division in Indian Ocean as Biden tours Middle East
  • Ukraine war: Hungary’s Orban says sanctions on Russia left European economy ‘gasping for air’
  • Technology is key to achieving Xi’s Jinping’s four goals for Hong Kong
  • ESG financiers need to first practise what they preach on climate change and net zero
  • China-Russia ‘no limits’ relationship should be taken with a pinch of salt
  • PetroChina expects 50-60 per cent jump in first-half profit on higher oil prices triggered by Russia-Ukraine war
  • BYD stock logs best rally since March after Tesla rival flags potential tripling in earnings as sales defy economic gloom
  • Copper sell-off deepens amid mounting concerns of a worldwide recession, dropping below US$7,000 for the first time since 2020
  • Thailand travel by train from Bangkok to Chiang Mai on the Northern Line offers visits to a series of former capitals – we hopped on board
  • Chris Patten’s Hong Kong Diaries of 1997 handover run-up take us behind the scenes as Britain prepared to take leave of its last colony
  • Anticipatory anxiety: what it is and how to overcome your fears, as cases rise with return to the office and going out after the pandemic
  • BTS’s J-Hope delves into his darker side with first solo album ‘Jack in the Box’
  • Malaysia’s ‘sex cult’ couple controversy escalates as police investigate comedy club owner
  • Summer reads 2022: 8 nonfiction and fiction books about change, from Atomic Habits to the I Ching
  • Hong Kong Film Awards 2022 preview: who will win, and who should win, the best film, director, actor and actress prizes
  • Dior CEO on new Paris store, LVMH head Bernard Arnault and why the ‘anti-metaverse’ is so important
  • Rosé wine isn’t all pastel pink and bland – artisan rosés have depth and complexity. But try telling the average drinker that
  • Michelin-star restaurant in the Arctic is the perfect gastronomic destination for luxury travel
  • Inside Ivana Trump’s complex relationship with Donald, who she once accused of sexual assault but backed all the way for president, and who she says used Twitter thanks to her
  • Where is Franklin Floyd, from Netflix’s Girl in the Picture, now? He abducted Suzanne Sevakis, married her, and was convicted for the murder of her colleague and son Michael Hughes ...
  • What teen tantrum? Shiloh Jolie-Pitt bonded with mum Angelina Jolie in Rome, hitting the shops and a Måneskin rock concert despite rift rumours – but why was Brad Pitt in Italy too?
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: Guardian: 16 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • New Zealand v Ireland: third and deciding rugby union Test – live!
  • Covid killed so many of us – now the government fears our tears and rage | Michael Rosen
  • Streaming: the best lost-in-the-wilderness films
  • Tories leadership hopefuls ‘scratching each other’s eyes out’ in race to No 10, says Labour– UK politics live
  • Gang warfare traps thousands in Haiti slum as fuel crisis add to desperation
  • Security tight as Sri Lankan MPs meet to elect new president amid first fuel arrival
  • Alison by Lizzy Stewart review – the making of an artist
  • No fairytale ending but magical memories as athletics queen Allyson Felix says farewell
  • What Hate Can Do: inside a devastating new exhibition on the Holocaust
  • The women who wish they weren’t mothers: ‘An unwanted pregnancy lasts a lifetime’
  • ‘Summer can be anxiety-inducing’: how parents cope with childcare
  • Steve Bannon admitted Trump ‘would lie about anything’, new book says
  • Brazil’s weak response to Phillips–Pereira murders threatens Indigenous people, lawyer says
  • No jobs, no money, no house: Ukrainians forced back to frontline towns
  • Trump, battered by January 6 testimony, mulls 2024 run – and not all Republicans are happy
  • Newt and the Never Trumpers: Gingrich, Tim Miller and the fate of the Republican party
  • Is it true there’s no such thing as a seagull? Try our kids’ quiz

  • Tamal Ray’s recipes for sesame and za’atar eggs and butter bean crudaiola

  • Buy now, try later: online clothing sales boom raises qualms over returns
  • Tim Dowling: my wife has seen the future … and it’s bungalow shaped
  • Blind date: ‘I’d once had a memorable encounter with his flatmate’
  • How to make radish pistou soup from both the bulbs and the leaves | Waste not
  • House January 6 panel subpoenas Secret Service for allegedly deleted text messages
  • Smiles and unity at the Pacific Islands Forum mask tough questions shelved for another day
  • Daughter of doctor who gave 10-year-old an abortion faced kidnapping threat

  • Elon Musk files motion against Twitter’s bid to fast-track trial

  • Fist bumps as Joe Biden arrives to reset ties with ‘pariah’ Saudi Arabia

  • ‘Impatient’ Cameron Smith sets Open pace with Rory McIlroy still in touch
  • England maintain perfect start as Russo double caps rout of Northern Ireland
  • Fitter, happier: England’s change of training tack has paid off in Australia
  • The Tory leadership debate: desperate as a sales pitch | John Crace
  • Sophia Dunkley makes history as England cruise past South Africa
  • Why is God a man? The woman who searched the world for a feminist religion
  • New Zealand seeks to repeat world-beating Covid response in face of surging cases
  • England braces for 40C temperatures as experts warn thousands could die
  • Texans settle claims with 30 women related to Deshaun Watson allegations
  • What happened in the Russia-Ukraine war this week? Catch up with the must-read news and analysis
  • US and Russia agree to fly each other’s astronauts to the ISS as tensions thaw
  • Canadian village entirely destroyed by wildfire in 2021 evacuated due to wildfire
  • Sri Lanka’s president Gotabaya Rajapaksa officially resigns
  • The next PM may well be from an ethnic minority. That’s a big deal – and a huge problem for Labour | Jonathan Freedland
  • Met police officer sacked after punching handcuffed black child in the face
  • Mads Pedersen wins stage 13 as Tour de France criticised over Covid and crowds
  • ‘Gas blackmail’: how Putin’s weaponised energy supplies are hurting Europe
  • ‘The years melted away’: long-lost friends on the joy of meeting up again
  • Paul Ryder, Happy Mondays bassist, dies aged 58
  • Roadrunner: A Film About Anthony Bourdain review – genius painted in broad strokes
  • Hundreds of teachers boycott Science Museum show over Adani sponsorship
  • ‘Deeply worrying’: fears for UK’s net zero goal under new Tory leader
  • Democrats are facing asymmetrical warfare. It’s time to wake up and fight back | Ben Davis
  • UN urged to move Cop27 from Egypt over ‘LGBTQ+ torture’
  • While Britain burns, the Tories are … fiddling with themselves again | Marina Hyde

entry.title: Asahi Shimbun: 16 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Thousands told to leave as record rain sets off Miyagi flooding
  • Suspect in Abe slaying tried to kill self so kin would get payout
  • Running out of cash, Yamagami decided in early July to kill Abe

  • VOX POPULI: Late ‘thinker of the rice paddy’ no fan of farm modernization
The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.
